1927, Mission James John opened their first Toddlers' Home Ferntree Gully (their first until 1932 when they leased Langley Hall, Bendigo).
Luke's capacity children aged months five years, housed groups according age.
School-aged children attended local schools, there kindergarten campus.
1976, Mission converted operations into family group homes decade units accommodating between seven children (mostly adolescents) Bendigo Eaglehawk.
1977, Church England Diocese Bendigo assumed auspice Luke's.
1979 Luke's Family Care Council managed multi-service agency providing range family support substitute care programs.
1981, Luke's Family Care operating early adolescent units Bendigo children aged years, regional foster care program, youth accommodation program (including youth residential unit eight young people, years), youth community placement program kindergarten children with special needs small groups.
Luke's also assumed responsibility Aidan's Children's Centre Bendigo's family group homes.
2016, organisation called Luke's Anglicare staff throughout central north-western Victoria delivering wide range services including intensive family services, out-of-home care services, youth accommodation support, residential care children.
